title: dryopteris scallanii
intro: Dryopteris scallanii, also known as Scallan's male fern or Scallan's fern, is a species of fern native to the British Isles. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds that have been found to have antioxidant, antimicrobial, and antiproliferative effects, contributing to its potential health benefits. In herbal preparations, the rhizome and fronds of Dryopteris scallanii are often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
